decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing Oath of Druids to get powerful creatures like Terravore onto the battlefield quickly. The deck also includes cards like Sphere of Resistance and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base includes Gemstone Mine, City of Brass, and Mox Diamond to ensure consistent mana production. Overall, the deck aims to establish board control with efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate while ramping into powerful threats and disrupting the opponent's strategy.

The deck aims to quickly cheat out Phyrexian Dreadnought with cards like Stifle and Flash of Insight, allowing for a powerful combo win condition. The deck also includes countermagic and card manipulation cards like Gush and Opt to protect the combo and draw into key pieces. Tsabo's Web and Chain of Vapor provide additional disruption and control options, while cards like Impulse and Peek help to dig deeper into the deck for combo pieces. The deck is focused on efficiently assembling and protecting the combo to win the game.

The deck focuses on putting pressure on the opponent with efficient, aggressive creatures like Nantuko Shade and Hypnotic Specter, backed up by disruption like Duress and Cabal Therapy. It also has removal options like Smother and Diabolic Edict to deal with threats. The deck aims to control the board and keep the opponent on the back foot, while slowly chipping away at their life total with evasive attackers. Additionally, it has a strong mana denial strategy with cards like Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's strategy while gradually building up a board presence with resilient threats. It utilizes a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and versatile creatures to maintain control of the game. Cards like Duress, Cabal Therapy, and Smother are used to disrupt the opponent's hand and board state, while creatures like Krosan Tusker and Blastoderm provide a clock for the opponent. Pernicious Deed acts as a versatile board wipe, allowing the deck to handle a wide range of threats.

The main concept of this deck revolves around quickly flooding the board with aggressive Goblin creatures, utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to bring out key threats while maintaining control with cards like Rishadan Port and Wasteland. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of Goblins, backed up by powerful support cards like Goblin Warchief and Siege-Gang Commander. The deck aims to apply constant pressure and finish off the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

The deck focuses on utilizing Survival of the Fittest to toolbox creatures in and out of the graveyard. It seeks to control the game by continuously generating value through creature synergy and disruption. The deck includes various mana dorks for acceleration, resilient creatures like Shrieking Drake and Squee, Goblin Nabob, as well as powerful finishers like Masticore and Kamahl, Fist of Krosa. The inclusion of Wirewood Symbiote and Intruder Alarm allows for additional synergies and combo potential, while cards like Tradewind Rider provide additional control elements. The deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents through strategic and versatile creature interactions.

The main concept of the deck is to control the game by using counterspells, removal spells, and land destruction while maintaining card advantage through Standstill and Fact or Fiction. The deck seeks to stall the game until it can establish a dominant board presence with powerful finishers like Exalted Angel and Decree of Justice. The deck also utilizes utility lands like Wasteland and Dust Bowl to disrupt opponents' mana bases. Overall, the deck aims to outlast and outvalue opponents through careful resource management and strategic decision-making.

The main focus of this deck is to establish a lock on the opponent using the Stasis combo, preventing them from untapping their permanents and effectively shutting down their ability to play spells. The deck includes several ways to control the opponent's board state, such as Counterspell and Chain of Vapor, while also incorporating card draw and disruption elements to maintain control of the game. The inclusion of cards like Black Vise and Thwart adds additional pressure on the opponent, forcing them to make difficult decisions and potentially leading to victory through attrition.

The main concept of this RG Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. The deck includes efficient creatures like Quirion Dryad and Mogg Fanatic, along with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate to control the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Call of the Herd and Treetop Village to provide additional threats and resilience. The goal is to apply constant pressure and win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana with cards like Mox Diamond and Gemstone Mine, while also disrupting the opponent's resources with Wasteland and Rishadan Port. The deck utilizes aggressive creatures like Wild Mongrel and Terravore to deal quick damage to the opponent, while also using land destruction cards like Armageddon to further control the game. The inclusion of Call of the Herd and Sylvan Library helps maintain card advantage and board presence. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early and efficiently with a combination of land destruction and aggressive creatures.

The deck focuses on using Stifle to counter key abilities or trigger effects while also utilizing cards like Phyrexian Dreadnought to quickly assemble a powerful combo. The deck also includes a number of control spells like Counterspell and Foil to protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to win the game through a quick and efficient combo while maintaining control over the game with counterspells and disruptive spells.

This deck focuses on generating mana quickly and efficiently in order to set up a powerful combo utilizing Ill-Gotten Gains and Brain Freeze to mill the opponent's library. The deck includes various cards that help dig for combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's strategy, while also including some protection in the form of Arcane Denial and Chain of Vapor. The deck aims to win the game by overwhelming the opponent with a flurry of spells that can lead to a quick victory through milling.

The main concept of this deck is to use Ill-Gotten Gains along with other spells like Brain Freeze and Dark Ritual to generate a powerful combo and win the game quickly. The deck also includes cards like Lotus Petal and Lion's Eye Diamond to ramp up mana quickly, as well as counterspells like Chain of Vapor and Cunning Wish to protect the combo. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent while assembling the combo pieces to quickly overwhelm them and secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game with powerful removal spells and countermagic, while maintaining card advantage and board presence through cards like Standstill and Fact or Fiction. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and win through incremental advantage and efficient use of resources. Key components include disruption with counterspells and removal, card draw and selection, and the ability to deploy threats like Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave to close out the game. The deck is designed to outlast and outvalue the opponent in a long game.

This deck focuses on leveraging Stasis to lock down the opponent's resources while using efficient countermagic and card draw to maintain control. The goal is to establish a board state where the opponent cannot untap their lands, forcing them into a slow and inevitable defeat. By disrupting their plans and controlling the tempo of the game, this deck aims to ensure victory through resource denial and chip damage from cards like Black Vise. Overall, the key strategy revolves around creating a lockdown situation that stifles the opponent's ability to play effectively.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Hermit Druid and Phyrexian Devourer to fill the graveyard with creatures and then use Animate Dead to bring them back and create a lethal Triskelion. The deck also includes various ways to find and protect the combo pieces, as well as disruption cards like Cabal Therapy and Meddling Mage to control the opponent's plays. By efficiently setting up and executing the combo, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.
